_meta.note: 18th major-arc enrichment dip; 2nd Class-D event cluster. 6 new nodes (no container tag — Riverlands/journey, matches S148-S150 A2 arcs). 44 edges. Spine threads the holdfast (was 0 causal) -> capture -> naming-gambit -> guards-killed -> fall -> iron-coin origin -> arya-departs-for-braavos (the marquee Harrenhal->Braavos origin seam, connecting to the S150 arc). Node bodies live in the mint script. The iron-coin GIFTED_TO re-cite (asos recollection -> acok-arya-09 actual giving) is applied in finalize.

edges.quote: his men had ridden all the way around the lake chasing Beric Dondarrion and slaying rebels
edges.note: Tywin-ordered chevauchee (Amory's column on the Beric-hunt around the Gods Eye) is the precondition that put the raiders in Yoren's path; Amory chose to storm the holdfast -> ENABLES. VERIFY node-fit: gregor-raids-the-riverlands era=prelude; confirm it spans into the ACOK campaign.

edges.quote: someone else slammed into her and dragged her to the ground, and a third man wrenched the sword from her grasp
edges.note: The lost battle directly produced Arya's capture (the marquee 0-causal-out fix on the hub). Coexists with the existing arya-captured SUB_BEAT_OF fight-at-the-holdfast.

edges.quote: They were being taken to serve Lord Tywin Lannister at Harrenhal, the Mountain told them.
edges.note: Captivity lands Arya at Harrenhal where Jaqen serves and owes her three deaths -- the precondition for the naming-gambit.

edges.quote: A girl might . . . name another name then, if a friend did help?
edges.note: Arya names Jaqen himself, then un-says it in exchange for him freeing the caged northmen -- the bargain that produces the weasel-soup dungeon massacre. Fixes guards-killed (had 0 causal-in).

edges.quote: Once freed, the captives stripped the dead guards of their weapons and darted up the steps with steel in hand.
edges.note: The weasel-soup freed the dungeon northmen who then took the castle -- the proximate enabling act of the fall. Coexists with the existing guards-killed SUB_BEAT_OF fall-of-harrenhal.

edges.quote: for I have duties too.
edges.note: Jaqen's debt paid + the castle fallen -> he departs, giving Arya the coin.

edges.quote: give that coin to any man from Braavos, and say these words to him—valar morghulis.
edges.note: MARQUEE ORIGIN SEAM: the coin + the words, given here, are what later enable Arya's passage to Braavos (the S150 arc). Completes Harrenhal->Braavos from the origin end.

edges.quote: I mean to give Harrenhal to Lord Vargo when I return to the north.
edges.note: Bolton's handover of Harrenhal to Vargo Hoat (and Arya to him) triggers Arya's escape decision.

edges.quote: Yoren tangled his black banner around his spike, and forced the point of his dirk through his armor while the man was fighting the cloth.
edges.note: Yoren fights in the battle where he dies (yoren DIED_AT gods-eye already exists).

edges.quote: She slashed down hard, and Needle’s castle-forged steel bit into the grasping fingers between the knuckles.
edges.note: Arya fights on the wall during the holdfast defense.

edges.quote: Winterfell!
edges.note: Needle wielded in the defense ("Winterfell!" she screamed as she cut the climber's fingers).

edges.quote: Ser Amory Lorch had given no more thought to burying his own dead than to those he had murdered
edges.note: Amory Lorch commanded the holdfast attack (his men, his dead). Distinct from gregor COMMANDS_IN arya-captured in baseline.

edges.quote: She stuck him with the pointy end, driving the blade upward with a wild, hysterical strength.
edges.note: Arya kills the stableboy -- Needle's first kill, her first human kill.

edges.quote: Needle went through his leather jerkin and the white flesh of his belly and came out between his shoulder blades.
edges.note: Needle is the named instrument (fixes needle's 0-outgoing).

edges.quote: The stableboy was dead, she’d killed him, and if he jumped out at her she’d kill him again.
edges.note: The first human kill shapes Arya toward lethal resolve (MOTIVATES -> character).

edges.quote: When he saw her looking, he lifted a hand to his face and laid two fingers casually against his cheek.
edges.note: Jaqen's two-finger salute = the second death (Weese) acknowledged; he is the agent (the dog the instrument). Jaqen's true means stays node-prose/theory-gated.

edges.quote: “Weese,” she whispered.
edges.note: Arya names Weese to Jaqen (parallels arya COMMANDS_IN chiswyck-dies in baseline).

edges.quote: Weese was sprawled across the cobbles, his throat a red ruin, eyes gaping sightlessly up at a bank of grey cloud.
edges.note: Weese is the victim (only weese DIED_AT harrenhal existed before).

edges.quote: Weese was sprawled across the cobbles, his throat a red ruin
edges.note: In the Harrenhal ward.

edges.quote: Arya lifted her gaze from the dead man and his dead dog.
edges.note: BORDERLINE: Arya sees the immediate aftermath (the body, the dog still feeding), not the kill-moment. Fresh-verify adjudicate keep-vs-drop.

edges.quote: Arya put her lips to his ear.
edges.note: Arya executes the gambit -- she is the sole agent.

edges.quote: Even in the burning barn, with walls of flame towering all around and him in chains, he had not seemed so distraught as he did now.
edges.note: Jaqen is the one named -- the target of the leverage; his distress proves the gambit landed.

edges.quote: She backed away from him, until she was pressed against the heart tree.
edges.note: The Harrenhal godswood.

edges.quote: He lifted her hand and pressed a small coin into her palm.
edges.note: Jaqen performs the gift.

edges.quote: He lifted her hand and pressed a small coin into her palm.
edges.note: Arya is the recipient of the gift + the words.

edges.quote: He lifted her hand and pressed a small coin into her palm.
edges.note: The coin is the artifact at the heart of the giving event.

edges.quote: give that coin to any man from Braavos, and say these words to him—valar morghulis.
edges.note: Jaqen reveals the coin's use + the words. The FM theology / valar-morghulis-as-religion stays node-prose (gated).

edges.quote: for I have duties too.
edges.note: The Harrenhal godswood/yard, after the fall.

edges.quote: Arya slid her dagger out and drew it across his throat, as smooth as summer silk.
edges.note: Arya plans and executes the escape, killing the gate guard. NB the weapon is Bolton's stolen dagger, NOT Needle (no needle WIELDED_IN here -- captured in node-prose).

edges.quote: Gendry was wearing oiled chainmail under his cloak, she saw, and he had his blacksmith’s hammer slung across his back.
edges.note: Gendry escapes with her (armed, with the horses).

edges.quote: Hot Pie’s red round face peered out from under a hood.
edges.note: Hot Pie is the third escapee (with bread and cheese).

edges.quote: There’s a guard on that postern,
edges.note: Through Harrenhal's postern gate.

edges.quote: In the dark the iron could pass for tarnished silver.
edges.note: The coin used as a decoy (drops it as fake silver to make the guard kneel) -- distinct from the giving + the later Braavos use.

edges.quote: A man pays his debts. A man owes three.
edges.note: The three-lives debt (Arya saved Jaqen/Rorge/Biter from the burning barn) drives Jaqen's killings for her -- the engine of the whole three-deaths thread.

edges.quote: Where was Lord Beric Dondarrion?
edges.note: The Tickler's interrogations' explicit purpose is to find Beric -- wires the D5 cluster to the Brotherhood-Without-Banners arc.

edges.quote: I mean to give Harrenhal to Lord Vargo when I return to the north.
edges.note: Roose holding Harrenhal positions him to march north to the Twins / the Red Wedding -- the highest-value WO5K seam from this cluster.

edges.quote: On your knees for the Lord of the Dreadfort!
edges.note: Roose Bolton as ACOK ruler of Harrenhal (post-fall). No qualifier (RULES is not a qualifier type).

edges.quote: Them Bloody Mummers killed some of Ser Amory’s lot in their beds, and the rest at table after they were good and drunk.
edges.note: The Brave Companions' mass-killing of the Lannister garrison is the proximate cause of the takeover (group-level complement to the baseline vargo-hoat BETRAYS amory-lorch).

edges.quote: the Hound for killing the butcher’s boy Mycah
edges.note: The Trident incident (Mycah's death) is a named origin of Arya's kill-list -- fixes incident-at-the-trident's 0-outgoing dead-end.

edges.quote: The queen wants him, old man, not that it’s your concern,
edges.note: Cersei issued the warrant driving the gold-cloak pursuit of Gendry (orderer who didn't personally execute).

edges.quote: He’s the one we want.
edges.note: Gendry is the target of the demand.

edges.quote: Yoren stepped out of the inn, fingering his tangled black beard.
edges.note: Yoren faces down the gold cloaks and refuses to give up Gendry.

edges.quote: Next time he won’t prance up and hand me no damn ribbon.
edges.note: The confrontation drives Yoren to push the band on (ride all night), the decision that shapes the journey.

edges.quote: Gendry had been sent to the forge; Arya seldom saw him.
edges.note: Gendry held as a captive smith at Harrenhal.
